findings.evidence: `rg "IsComplexLinearMap\.(add|neg|sub|smul)|isComplexLinearMap_(add|neg|sub|smul)" TauCeti/Geometry/Symplectic/AlmostComplex.lean TauCeti/Geometry/Symplectic/JHolomorphic.lean` finds no such API; the current users rewrite through `isComplexLinearMap_iff_mem_complexLinearMaps` at `JHolomorphic.lean:333`, `342`, `353`, and `362`.
findings.fix: Add public lemmas such as `IsComplexLinearMap.add`, `.neg`, `.sub`, and `.smul`/`.const_smul` next to `zero`, `id`, and `comp`, and use them from the `JHolomorphic` closure proofs.
findings.issue: `IsComplexLinearMap` still has no direct `add`/`neg`/`sub`/real-`smul` closure lemmas, despite the PR making real-linear combinations part of the public `J`-holomorphic API.

summary: The `J`-holomorphic closure lemmas are exposed, but the linear-map predicate they depend on is missing the matching public operation API. Consumers of the new continuous-linear-map iff are forced through the implementation submodule instead of using `IsComplexLinearMap` directly.
